summary: A Go-based monolithic application featuring a test runner, schema validation, and MCP handlers, heavily utilizing automated agent-driven test generation.
concerns: Highly fragmented test generation history suggests potential test bloat or redundancy
Heavy reliance on automated agent-generated tests may reduce test intentionality
Multiple overlapping test directories for internal packages could cause maintenance confusion
suggestions: Consolidate and deduplicate the agent-generated extra tests to ensure they provide unique value
Review the core runner and MCP handler logic for integration coverage gaps
Establish clear boundaries for automated test generation to prevent unmanaged growth
